narrative.narrative: Extreme fear is gripping the market as the Fear & Greed Index plunges to 25, mirroring the capitulation vibe of August 2024, yet stablecoin reserves are quietly expanding—signaling dry powder is accumulating on the sidelines. This identical setup of panic coinciding with rising buying power previously marked the local bottom before a sharp relief rally. The current risk-on regime suggests the anxiety is a sentiment trap rather than a structural breakdown.
narrative.headline: Silent Accumulation Beneath the Panic

consensus.key_reasoning: Fear & Greed at 25 places the market in the Panic band (24-31), but the analyst's Anxiety call (39-46) is within the legal ±1 rung window and reflects the divergent, choppy structure better than a pure panic label. Both brains agree on a risk-on LONG bias supported by rising stablecoin reserves (dry powder accumulation) against extreme fear, with the 80% August 2024 bottom analog reinforcing the setup, though the analyst's low confidence and 8 divergent factors warrant restraint from an aggressive stance.
disagreements_noted: Analyst labels phase Anxiety (39-46 band) while Narrative frames sentiment as capitulation/panic (F&G=25, Panic band 24-31) — a one-rung gap that is legal but reflects different reads on whether structural breakdown or sentiment trap is dominant.

Analyst confidence is moderate-low (0.55) with 8 divergent factors (ETF flows, MVRV Z-score, VIX, BTC momentum, dominance fade, funding, DXY weakness, gold strength) while Narrative confidence is high (0.8) treating divergences as accumulation signals rather than warning signs.
Analyst verdict is DIVERGING (mixed structural picture) while Narrative presents a cohesive bottoming story — the analyst's factor pattern read tempers the Narrative's bullish conviction.
